blocks.content: As they entered the command headquarters building and the iron door closed,
the cheers from outside were cut off as if waking from a dream.
The lights on the ceiling of the hallway maintained minimal brightness,
and soldiers stood guard at precise intervals along the path.
Armed with the first stage of the Mecha combat system, they never glanced at Shirone's group.
However, their gaze was intense enough to pierce through walls.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They're all elite soldiers.
The Mecha tribe may not have superhuman physical abilities or magical powers, but they have the technology to compensate for both.
In war, winning is all that matters, so it would be a mistake to think their power is weak just because their physical strength is.
A colonel guided Shirone's group to an underground bunker.
The bunker's security system was not inferior to the association's technology, and when the iron door opened, numerous machines were operating.
Technicians were busy moving their hands while monitoring screens, and a man in a commander's cloak stood with his hands behind his back, observing their work.

blocks.speaker: Colonel
blocks.content: Commander, I've brought Shirone.
At the colonel's words, the commander turned around.
He was a man in his late twenties with a long scar running from his temple to his ear.
His thin lips were stubbornly closed, and his eyes behind his glasses showed both coldness and passion.
Rebel 1st Commander Crude.
As Kanya had mentioned, he was a young man.
In war, especially in a situation where defeat seemed imminent, age didn't matter much.
What soldiers wanted was a commander who combined the bravery to charge towards death first and the tactical skill to save as many people as possible.
Crude was a man who met those conditions exactly.

A genius pilot who knew everything about the maintenance, driving, and operation of the Titan, which belonged to the third stage of the Mecha combat system, and a war hero of the Mecha tribe who had defeated a two-horned Mara.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Are you Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm Shirone.
Crude extended his hand to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Nice to meet you. I'm Crude, the 1st Commander of the rebels.
Crude carefully observed Shirone's group.
With his extensive combat experience, he recognized their strength at a glance, but his confident gaze did not waver.
However, when he saw Plu, his gaze lingered for a moment, returning to the twenty-eight-year-old engineer who had wrestled with machines all his life.
A petite woman with blue hair braided down to her waist, exuding a sharp and decisive style.
What particularly caught his attention was her eyes. She was the only one in the group whose eyes were full of determination.
Like a woman warrior going to her first battle.
But that was it.
Crude also knew the story of Shirone, the light of Sector 73, but the reality of war was too harsh to rely on myths.
Let's go somewhere quiet.
Crude led everyone to a strategy room deep within the bunker.
Naturally, no tea was served, and they got straight to the point as soon as they sat down.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: I heard you wanted to meet me?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: I refuse.
Crude's answer was immediate.
I heard there was friction with the battalion commander. Of course, a soldier whose spirit is broken is useless to us as well, but thinking rationally, there's no chance of winning a war right now.
Thinking that you can attack Heaven just because you subdued giants who lost their identity is nothing but a delusion.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: So, you haven't given up on the war.

We always leave room for a truce or compromise. I can't let those who trust and follow me die in vain. But I don't think Heaven sees us as a subject for compromise. If we have to fight, we must win to survive.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: What if we have a strategy to win?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Hmph, nonsense.
Even with the addition of the heretics and the citizens from Heaven, the tens of thousands of rebels had only a handful of notable victories against Heaven.
The alliance of Nor and Mecha couldn't even capture a three-horned Mara, let alone a Heavenly Angel. What could a mere ten humans do?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: We will paralyze the Aegis system with Tagis. If we do that, we can infiltrate Heaven and conduct guerrilla warfare.
The Aegis, Heaven's core air defense system, is an impregnable shield that determines interception order with two hundred search methods and can attack seven thousand targets simultaneously.
If they could break through it and enter without bloodshed, it would bring a ray of hope to the rebels.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Hahaha.
Crude covered his face and shook with laughter.
I don't know where you heard about Tagis, but it's already a lost technology in Mecha.
When he lowered his hand, he was no longer laughing.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Think logically. There's no way the angels would leave a technology that can paralyze Heaven's interception system alone.
To revive the technology, you'd need to access the Akashic Records. But to do that, you'd first have to break through the Aegis system. Do you get it now? The order is reversed, idiot.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What if... I have that technology?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: What?
What nonsense is that?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Just because it's lost in Heaven doesn't mean it's lost in the land of Earth.
And I have the perfectly restored design of Tagis in my head.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: How about now? Does it make sense?
Crude's eyes began to show confusion.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: What if you're lying...

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: I can restore it right now. I have no forgetfulness in my head.
You're an engineer too, so you'll understand when you see the blueprint.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So that's why... the Black Line...
Shirone saw Sein in a new light.
Ancient weapons were top secrets in any kingdom.
He had risked his life, facing execution just for accessing the information, all to save Miro.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Hmm...
Crude was now confused too.
Who were these people? Why were they trying to do what tens of thousands of rebels couldn't?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, the important thing is whether it will succeed.
Even if they paralyzed Aegis and entered Heaven, angels, Maras, giants, and fairies awaited them.
Penetrating to Aravot, where Anke La was, would be like carrying gunpowder and jumping into a fire.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But it's worth a try. Even if there's only a one percent chance.
Was this a miracle? Or the last desperate struggle of a candle burning brightest before it goes out?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: There are three... conditions.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Let's hear them.
Crude counted on his fingers.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: First, use Shirone, the light of Sector 73, to persuade the 2nd Headquarters.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Of course. What's next?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Second, even if you have the blueprint, we can't manufacture a large Tagis that affects all of Heaven here.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Alright. What's the last condition?
For the first time, Crude hesitated to propose.
No matter how great Shirone's team was, it wasn't something a single team could do.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: To manufacture Tagis, you'll probably need Black Elixir.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Black Elixir?
Most of Heaven's creatures generate Elixir by absorbing the Life Stream flowing through the planet.
There are five types: Red, Green, Yellow, White, and finally, Black.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Does it have to be Black? From what I know, Elixir can be bypassed with material mixing and ratios.
Each type of Elixir has unique effects. Red generates heat, Green involves biochemical elements, Yellow changes energy properties, and White interacts with atmospheric elements.
Heaven's rocks are so durable they don't weather even after tens of thousands of years. That's why we need Elixir, which contains the planet's power.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: For example, to cast the metal for one Guroi, you need eight thousand Red Elixirs, but mixing in White Elixir greatly increases heat reaction efficiency.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Then what does Black Elixir do?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Black Elixir, simply put, governs time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Time? Like magic?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: No. To be precise, it reverses or accelerates irreversible phenomena.
If you use Black Elixir on a liquid mixed with ink and water, it separates back into ink and water.
If you have a burn, using Black and Green Elixir can repair cell damage.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's... incredible.
It defied the chemical laws he had learned in school.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: What about acceleration?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: It shortens reaction time.
Green Elixir is the basic element of living things, and mixing it with Black Elixir can cause mutations.
In some cases, it produces evolutionary results thousands of years ahead.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Philosopher's Stone.
Shirone had heard of it before.
Though not commonly used in magic, it was a substance known as the dream material for alchemists.
A fantastical substance that could turn mere iron into gold and cure incurable diseases, existing in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could it be...
A particular incident came to Shirone's mind.
When Kanya's mother became a target of the elixir's magic, a large glass sphere filled with black liquid had appeared.
A horrifying spell that dissolved humans and cast them into giant molds to create giants.
What made it even more terrifying was that it was an inexplicable phenomenon to his mind.
A black liquid that dissolved and reconstituted living beings.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: You're saying we can use the Philosopher's Stone to shorten the production process of Tagis?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Exactly.
Tagis can be made with existing Elixirs, but the process of melting rocks to extract and fuse metals would take too much time.
We provide the equipment. Whether it's Guroi or anything else, we melt everything with Black Elixir to process the metals needed for Tagis.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: If we collect it through hunting, what are the chances?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Zero percent.
Black Elixir isn't generated in living bodies. It's an element that can only be obtained from beings that have transcended the flow of time, namely the dead.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Given that different creatures collect planetary energy differently, the abnormality of the Philosopher's Stone made sense.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: So what are we supposed to do?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: There's one place. A 30 percent, no, a 50 percent chance of obtaining Black Elixir.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Where is that?
Crude hesitated again.
But realizing he couldn't stop now, he opened his eyes wide and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: The world of the dead, Niflheim.
ΓÇö
